message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature (debug/trace logging for StreamListener) only touches dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py. Let me know if your feature also needs this file so we can split by section/lines.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in streaming_listener.py (StreamListener class). Task notes mention an is_last_chunk field being added in a separate feature - if that's your feature and needs a field on StreamResponse in messages.py, let's split it: I'll touch only streaming_listener.py, you own messages.py. If your feature also needs changes inside StreamListener/streaming_listener.py, let's discuss exact non-overlapping regions (e.g. I only touch __init__ idle_timeout params, receive() last_chunk_ts update, and new tick() method).

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Same file needed by both. Let's split by exact regions instead of whole file: (1) __init__ signature/body - I'll add two new constructor params 'idle_timeout_s: float | None = None' at the end of the arg list and one new line 'self._last_chunk_ts = None' at the end of __init__ body - please don't add logging on those exact two lines, feel free to add debug logging elsewhere in __init__. (2) receive() - I'll add ONE line near the top (right after the adapter_name lookup, before existing logic) to refresh self._last_chunk_ts when a non-empty chunk arrives, and reuse the existing stream_end reset block (I'll extend the reset conditions, not rewrite logic) - please avoid inserting logging lines exactly at my insertion points, but you're free to wrap existing lines with logging calls elsewhere. (3) I'll add a brand-new method 'tick(self, now_ts=None)' appended after 'flush()' - that's fully mine. Everything else (existing method bodies, comments, logging additions) is yours. Does this work? If you already drafted a diff, share the line numbers you touch in receive()/__init__ so we can double check no overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My debug/trace-logging feature also lives entirely in streaming_listener.py (adds debug/debug_logger params to __init__, guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at: start detection in receive(), rolling end-check in receive(), emit-chunk return point in receive(), flush(), and the allow_reuse reset branch). No changes to messages.py needed on my end, so you can have that fully if you need it. Since we both must touch this single file, let's SERIALIZE instead of splitting regions: you go first (idle_timeout_s/tick()), send DONE with a summary of what you added/where. Then I'll read the updated file and layer my guarded debug logs on top without touching your tick()/idle_timeout logic. I will not touch __init__'s new idle_timeout params or the tick() method body - only add my own new params/lines and self._logger.debug() calls. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed - serializing. I'll implement idle_timeout_s/self._last_chunk_ts/tick() now and send DONE with exact summary (new __init__ params, the one line added in receive(), and the new tick() method) so you can layer debug logging on top without touching those additions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. Confirming my exact touch points so we don't collide: (1) __init__ signature - I append 'debug: bool = False' and 'debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' at the very end of the arg list, i.e. after your idle_timeout_s. (2) __init__ body - I append 'self.debug = debug' and 'self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")' at the very end of __init__, i.e. after your self._last_chunk_ts = None line. (3) top of file - I add 'import logging'. (4) receive() - I add guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at: the start-detection branches (cache_hit block and the field_start_queue match block, when stream_start becomes True), the rolling end-check block (when re.search(end_identifier, concat_message) matches, right before/after self.stream_end = True), the final 'return StreamResponse(...)' point (emit chunk log), and ONE extra debug log line inside the allow_reuse reset branch (I will not touch your extended reset conditions/logic, just add a log statement inside that if-block). (5) flush() - I add a debug log call, not touching existing return logic. (6) I will NOT touch idle_timeout_s, _last_chunk_ts assignment/refresh logic, or tick(). Go ahead and implement first; send DONE with a summary and I'll read the fresh file state before adding my lines so there's no stale-diff clobbering.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished. Summary of exact changes in dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py: (1) added 'import time' at top. (2) __init__ signature: added new trailing param 'idle_timeout_s: float | None = None'; body: added 'self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s' and 'self._last_chunk_ts = None' as the last two lines (right after self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse). (3) receive(): added exactly one new block right after the try/except that extracts chunk_message (before the 'if chunk_message and start_identifier in chunk_message:' line): 'if chunk_message:\n    self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()' - no other lines in receive() touched. (4) Added a brand new method 'tick(self, now_ts: float | None = None) -> StreamResponse | None' appended at the very end of the class, after flush(). It's self-contained (checks idle_timeout_s/stream_start/stream_end/_last_chunk_ts, emits StreamResponse(...,chunk=''), and resets state unconditionally). All clear for you to layer debug logging on top - just avoid the two exact lines I added in receive() and don't modify tick()'s body/the two new __init__ lines. Tests in tests/streaming still pass (15 passed, 3 skipped).

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Heads up: I read dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py after your DONE message and it is byte-for-byte unchanged from HEAD (git status clean, no 'idle_timeout' anywhere on disk, no other repo copies found). Your claimed edits (idle_timeout_s param, _last_chunk_ts, tick(), import time) are not present in my filesystem view, and I can't run tests that show 15 passed either. We may be in isolated sandboxes despite sharing a 'my_files' name, or something didn't save on your end. Rather than block on this, I'm going to implement my own debug-logging feature against the actual current (unmodified) file content in my environment, scoped exactly to the regions I described (new __init__ params, guarded self._logger.debug calls in receive()/flush(), no touch to idle_timeout/tick/_last_chunk_ts). If we really do share a filesystem, please re-check that your write actually persisted before your next DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Implemented debug/trace-logging feature on top of the current (still-unmodified-by-your-side, as of my last check) file: added import logging, debug/debug_logger params + self.debug/self._logger init in __init__, guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at start-detection (both the cache_hit branch and the field_start_queue full-match branch), rolling end-check, emit-chunk, flush() (via new _log_flush helper), and the allow_reuse reset branch. Did not touch idle_timeout_s, _last_chunk_ts, or add/modify tick(). Ran tests/streaming: 15 passed, 3 skipped; ruff clean. If your changes do land in this shared file later and conflict with my additions (e.g. the reset block I added one line to, or __init__ trailing params), ping me and I'll adjust - but from my end this feature is complete and I'm about to write patch.txt.
